title: Pre-Imaging Liquid Growth Medium - Yeast
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ei8c4","text":"This protocol describes the steps to prepare liquid culture medium for Saccharomyces cerevisiae. This liquid medium is used to optimize yeast cultures for fluorescence imaging by the reduction of auto-fluorescence through an abundance of Adenine [1].","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Fill a 500 ml flask with 445 mL ddH 2 O. Add a magnetic stirring bar and place the flask on a stirring hot plate.
Add 3.36 g Yeast Nitrogen Base with Ammonium Sulfate without Amino Acids (YNB)

Add 0.86 g SC-XX powder. In regard to cover all optional dropout media the amino acid base holds the notification -xx, where xx stand for the amino acid(s) that is as selection factor, missing in the medium.
Add 5 mL Adenine 100x solution. The additional Adenine is supposed to repress the Adenine synthesize to reduce a possible accumulation of red pigment [1].
Autoclave for 00:15:00 at 121 °C . Remove the stirring bar before going to autoclave.
When the medium cooled down to around 80 °C add 50 mL sterile Glucose 20%.
The medium can be store at the bench for 2 to 3 months.
Fill a 500 ml flask with 320 mL ddH 2 O. Add a magnetic stirring bar and place the flask on a stirring hot plate.
Add 25 mL YNB 20x solution (Yeast Nitrogen Base with Ammonium Sulfate without Amino Acids).

Add 100 mL SC-XX 10x solution. In regard to cover all optional dropout media the amino acid base holds the notification -xx, where xx stand for the amino acid(s) that is as selection factor, missing in the medium.
Add 5 mL Adenine 100x solution. The additional Adenine is supposed to repress the Adenine synthesize to reduce a possible accumulation of red pigment [1].
Autoclave for 00:15:00 at 121 °C . Remove the stirring bar before going to autoclave.
When the medium cooled down to around 80 °C add 50 mL sterile Glucose 20%.
The medium can be store at the bench for 2 to 3 months.
creator: Mathias Hammer
